cases.indexTitle: How companies from different industries work in Taska
cases.indexSubtitle: Short scenarios: typical context, what we automate and what result the team gets. Choose an industry closer to your business.
cases.fitness.title: Case Study Fitness Clubs & Studios
cases.fitness.lead: Subscriptions, coaches, schedules and sales — when the flow of customers is high, it is important not to lose leads and discipline of the hall.
cases.fitness.forWho: Networks and single clubs, studios, swimming pools, where there is a sales department, administrators and service.
cases.fitness.pain1: Leads from social networks and from the site do not reach the record on the trial — there is no single funnel.
cases.fitness.pain2: The schedule and replacement of trainers are agreed in chats, clients receive conflicting information.
cases.fitness.pain3: It is difficult for a manager to see the conversion to a subscription and loading in the halls.
cases.fitness.b1: Funnel from application to payment with tasks to administrator and trainer.
cases.fitness.b2: Customer card: visits, subscription, communication history.
cases.fitness.b4: Sales and load reports for the manager and marketing.
cases.fitness.out1: Fewer lost leads and a more transparent customer path to the subscription.
cases.fitness.out2: Less chaos in the schedule and communication with the hall.
cases.fitness.out3: Management decisions are based on data, not on feelings.
cases.fitness.ex1: The client wrote on Instagram — the manager conducts the transaction before paying for the trial and full subscription.
cases.fitness.ex2: Coach replacement: administration task and notification to the group in an agreed format.
cases.fitness.ex3: Monthly promotion: a template of tasks for marketing, hall and sales.
cases.production.title: Case study: production and workshop
cases.production.lead: Order queue, raw materials, stages and shipment — when the bottleneck is not obvious, deadlines and margins are lost.
cases.production.forWho: Small and medium-sized production, assembly, food production, where there is a workshop, supply and sales department.
cases.production.pain1: The priority of orders changes "over the phone", the shop and office live in different versions of the truth.
cases.production.pain2: Marriage and alterations are not tied to the stages — the same mistakes are repeated.
cases.production.pain3: The supply learns about the needs late, the purchases disrupt the production plan.
cases.production.b1: Production order as a chain of tasks by sections.
cases.production.b2: Link to material requisitions and delivery statuses.
cases.production.b3: Accounting for defects with responsible and corrective actions.
cases.production.b4: Reports on deadlines and issue for the manager.
cases.production.out1: Predictable terms and less downtime between stages.
cases.production.out2: The reasons for marriage and bottlenecks are more transparent.
cases.production.out3: Coordination of supply and workshop without unnecessary meetings.
cases.production.ex1: A large order is divided into batches, each section marks readiness.
cases.production.ex2: A defect in quality control creates a task for analyzing the cause and adjusting the process.
cases.production.ex3: The weekly shipment plan is synchronized with the production queue.
cases.marketing.title: Case: marketing and creative agencies
cases.marketing.lead: Projects with deadlines, approvals and edits - when everything is in correspondence, margins and reputation suffer.
cases.marketing.forWho: Performance agency, SMM, branding, production studio with several clients and teams.
cases.marketing.pain1: Briefs and edits are scattered across chats, versions of technical specifications do not match.
cases.marketing.pain2: It is unclear who bottlenecked: account, design or account finance.
cases.marketing.pain3: Additional work has not been formalized - the project margin is distorted.
cases.marketing.b1: The project as a single place: stages, files, responsible persons.
cases.marketing.b2: Tasks for approval and deadlines with reminders.
cases.marketing.b3: Linking with accounts and payments to control margins.
cases.marketing.b4: The team's upload is visible to the manager without Excel.
cases.marketing.out1: There are fewer missed deadlines and fewer arguments about “what was agreed upon.”
cases.marketing.out2: Transparent margin on projects and accounting for additional work.
cases.marketing.out3: More equal load distribution between accounts and production.
cases.marketing.ex1: New client: template of stages from brief to delivery with tasks by role.
cases.marketing.ex2: A major edit from a client is documented as an approved addition to the invoice.
cases.marketing.ex3: Weekly status for all active projects in one report.
cases.it.title: Case: IT and product teams
cases.it.lead: Sprints, backlog, releases and support - when tickets and business tasks are in different places, priority is lost.
cases.it.forWho: Outsource development, product teams, integrators, where there is project sales and setting development tasks.
cases.it.pain1: Requests from clients and internal tasks compete without a single prioritization.
cases.it.pain2: Sales promise deadlines without visibility of the team's workload.
cases.it.pain3: Post-release support is sparse, there are no clear SLAs for response.
cases.it.b1: A single task board with priorities and responsibilities.
cases.it.b2: Connection of transactions and projects with putting into development.
cases.it.b3: Process templates for release and acceptance.
cases.it.b4: Reports on workload and overdues for the team lead and CEO.
cases.it.out1: More predictable deliveries and fewer “time vs quality” conflicts.
cases.it.out2: Sales and production see the same loading picture.
cases.it.out3: Post-release support with clear queues and responsible people.
cases.it.ex1: New contract for revision - stages, assessment and tasks for the team.
cases.it.ex2: Release: checklist for testing and approval with the client.
cases.it.ex3: An incident from a client is queued with priority and escalation.
cases.delivery.title: Case: delivery service and couriers
cases.delivery.lead: Orders, routes, statuses and returns - when everything is in calls, the client and the office see a different reality.
cases.delivery.forWho: Courier services, last mile, B2B delivery, where there is a call center, warehouse and drivers.
cases.delivery.pain1: The status of the order is clarified by calls, the client is dissatisfied with the transparency.
cases.delivery.pain2: Delays and returns are not understood for reasons - the same failures are repeated.
cases.delivery.pain3: It is difficult to assess the effectiveness of couriers and routes.
cases.delivery.b1: An order as a chain of statuses with tasks for each role.
cases.delivery.b2: Notifications to the client and internal alerts in case of deviations.
cases.delivery.b3: Accounting for returns and disputes with the person responsible.
cases.delivery.out1: Increased customer trust due to predictable statuses.
cases.delivery.out2: Less chaos with delays and returns.
cases.delivery.out3: The basis for optimizing routes and bonuses for couriers.
cases.delivery.ex1: The order has been accepted - the warehouse collects it, the courier is on the way, the client receives a status in the messenger.
cases.delivery.ex2: The delay at the point is a task for the dispatcher and a transparent update of the ETA.
cases.delivery.ex3: The return is processed through a process with warehouse and finance.
cases.household.title: Case: household services
cases.household.lead: Calling a technician, visiting, spare parts and payment - when there are a lot of requests, it is important not to lose contact and the timing of the departure.
cases.household.forWho: Equipment repair, cleaning, plumbing, electrical, air conditioning installation and similar field services.
cases.household.pain1: Applications from the website and phone are duplicated, technicians go to the wrong addresses.
cases.household.pain2: Spare parts and visits are not agreed upon - the client waits again.
cases.household.pain3: The manager cannot see the conversion of applications and the workload of teams.
cases.household.b1: A single application funnel with address, window and type of work.
cases.household.b2: Tasks for the foreman and supply with checklists.
cases.household.b3: Invoices and payments are linked to the order.
cases.household.b4: Reports on visits and repeated requests.
cases.household.out1: Less loss of applications and delays in addresses and time.
cases.household.out2: More transparent procurement of spare parts and visits.
cases.household.out3: Network scaling without loss of service quality.
cases.household.ex1: The client left a request - the dispatcher appointed a technician, the client received confirmation of the window.
cases.household.ex2: You need a spare part - a purchasing task with blocking exit until arrival.
cases.household.ex3: Repeated customer contact opens the history of past orders.
